title: Array.forEach/map ignore async callbacks: code after the loop runs before the work finishes
rule: Array.prototype.forEach/map/filter are not async-aware: they call an async callback, receive a Promise, and either discard it (forEach) or collect it unawaited (map/filter yield Promise[] and filter even keeps every element since a Promise is truthy). The method returns synchronously, so code after the loop — and the enclosing function's return — runs before the awaited work finishes, and rejections go unhandled. For sequential async work use for...of with await; for concurrent use await Promise.all(items.map(asyncFn)). Never rely on await inside .forEach.
triggers: results are empty right after a forEach with async callback
code after forEach runs before await finishes
array is [] but populated later in async loop
await inside forEach has no effect
map returns array of promises instead of values
